headline: Nebius Group’s AI Mega Deals And Insider Sales Raise Investor Questions
summary: Nebius Group (NasdaqGS:NBIS) has entered into multi year, multi billion dollar infrastructure agreements with Meta and Nvidia, including a $27b partnership with Meta. Nvidia has committed a reported $2b investment tied to Nebius Group's AI cloud build out and hardware deployments. The company is pursuing an accelerated AI data center expansion across Europe focused on multi gigawatt capacity. Following these announcements, a cluster of insider stock sales by top Nebius executives has been...

headline: Nvidia-Backed Firmus Raises $505 Million As AI Data Center Boom Accelerates
summary: Nvidia Corp backed Australian AI infrastructure startup Firmus has secured $505 million in fresh equity funding, pushing its valuation to $5.5 billion. AI Infrastructure Demand Drives Fresh Capital The latest funding round was led by Coatue, with participation from Nvidia. The investment remains subject to closing conditions, Nikkei Asia reported on Tuesday. This marks Firmus' third equity raise in six months, bringing total capital raised during that period to $1.35 billion. The funding arrives

headline: Nvidia’s Lunar AI And Cybersecurity Push Deepen Its Critical Infrastructure Role
summary: Nvidia is extending its Jetson edge AI platform to the Moon through a new collaboration with Firefly Aerospace to enable real time AI data processing in lunar orbit. The company is also joining Project Glasswing, an urgent multi company effort to apply advanced AI models to emerging cybersecurity threats. NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) is moving its AI footprint beyond traditional data centers, with Jetson hardware and software now aimed at on orbit analytics around the Moon. For investors tracking...

headline: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
summary: Rene Haas, the CEO of chip designer Arm, is in line to lead much of parent ‌SoftBank's international business while keeping his current role at Arm, ‌the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.  SoftBank did not ​immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters, while Arm, which is majority-owned by the Japanese conglomerate, declined to comment.  Haas's appointment aims to push forward ‌SoftBank's AI chip strategy, ⁠Project Izanagi, launched to compete with rivals such as U.S.-based chipmaker Nvidia, according to the FT report.

content.title: Arm CEO Haas in line to lead much of parent SoftBank's international business, FT reports
content.summary: Rene Haas, the CEO of chip designer Arm, is in line to lead much of parent ‌SoftBank's international business while keeping his current role at Arm, ‌the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.  SoftBank did not ​immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters, while Arm, which is majority-owned by the Japanese conglomerate, declined to comment.  Haas's appointment aims to push forward ‌SoftBank's AI chip strategy, ⁠Project Izanagi, launched to compete with rivals such as U.S.-based chipmaker Nvidia, according to the FT report.

thumbnail.caption: Malaysia PM announces $250 million deal with Arm Holdings for chip design blueprints
